question: Which adjective describes someone who is 'determined to get what they want'?
My sister and I _____ always falling out when we were teenagers.
Which phrase means 'to criticise someone'?
Read: 'In England during the 1800s, many people came to the cities to work. There were places called workhouses where the poorest people could live, work and eat.' Why did people go to workhouses?
choices: to live, work and eat in order to survive
to meet their friends and family

question: He _____ be tiny, but now he's quite a bit taller than his brother. (past state that no longer exists)
Which adjective means 'not talking in a proud way about your achievements'?
Husam has lost his phone. He is very absent-minded. (Combine using a relative clause)
choices: Husam, which is very absent-minded, has lost his phone.
Husam, who is very absent-minded, has lost his phone.
Husam, whose is very absent-minded, has lost his phone.
Husam, where is very absent-minded, has lost his phone.

question: Which phrase means 'to try and impress somebody'?
Read: 'Oliver was shocked when he saw the boys take things from people's pockets. He understood that they were thieves!' Why was Oliver shocked?
choices: because the boys were running fast
because he saw the boys stealing from people
because Fagin was angry at the boys
because the police were chasing him

question: We use a present participle to replace a relative clause with _____ verb.
Which word means 'a noisy argument or fight'?
He's quite _____ — you would never know that he's a big star.
In 'Broadly speaking, Generation Z have an entrepreneurial spirit', the phrase 'broadly speaking' is used to:
The land _____ the house is built is extremely picturesque.
Which phrase means 'to react as if what happened is much worse than it is'?
She's such a(n) _____ teacher that all her students want to be like her.
Read: 'Fagin then told Oliver to go into the streets with the other boys and learn from them.' What did Fagin want Oliver to learn?
When I was young, I _____ visit my grandparents every weekend. (repeated past action)
Which phrase means 'to get angry'?
What does 'huddled together' mean?
choices: standing very far apart from each other
sat or stood very close to other people

question: Rewrite using a participle clause: 'The copy which was made by the carbon paper was called the carbon copy.'
choices: The copy making by the carbon paper was called the carbon copy.
The copy made by the carbon paper was called the carbon copy.
The copy which made by carbon paper was called the carbon copy.
The copy to make by carbon paper was called the carbon copy.

question: Which adjective means 'intolerant of other people's beliefs and practices'?

Read: 'Mr Brownlow explained that Oliver's mother was a rich woman. But his brother, a man called Monks, knew that Oliver would only get the money if Oliver was an honest person.' Why did Monks want Oliver to become a thief?
choices: because Monks was a kind person who wanted to help Oliver
so that Oliver could never be honest and Monks could keep all the money
because Monks wanted Oliver to leave the country
so that Oliver could give the money to Mr Brownlow

question: In which type of relative clause can we use 'that' instead of 'which' or 'who'?
Which phrase means 'to reconcile with someone after a disagreement'?
He's a very _____ person and will always act in the proper way.
What does 'a sense of belonging' mean?
choices: feeling comfortable and accepted in a particular place

question: My brother _____ always show off, even now.
Which phrase means 'to speak badly about someone when they are not present'?
Read: 'There were places called workhouses where the poorest people could live, work and eat.' The relative clause 'where the poorest people could live, work and eat' is:
Which adjective describes a person who 'behaves in a way that shows they think someone is criticising them'?
What does 'flunking an important exam' mean?
choices: passing an exam with high marks

question: She will look after your baby well; she's reliable, responsible and completely _____.
Read: 'Going online can be seen as a way of bringing families together as well as a distraction.' What TWO roles does going online play?
choices: it brings families together and it distracts them
it distracts families and causes arguments

question: When I was little, my parents _____ forever telling me off about the things I'd done.
Which phrase means 'to feel what another person is feeling'?
The man _____ daughter I met in Jordan is my teacher.
In non-defining relative clauses, we _____ use 'that'.
Which word describes 'a child whose parents have died'?
In 'To some extent, electronic devices are a distraction in the classroom', the phrase 'to some extent' means:
